# Comp110 For All!

<!-- This is a comment. Below, you'll see code for inserting an image. To make this image appear, update <custom-path>. To add an image, save it inside the imgs folder of this repository. -->
<img src="/data-project/static/imgs/image.png" alt="Video Desire Distribution" width="500"/>
<img src="/data-project/static/imgs/image2.png" alt="Understanding vs Video Need" width="500"/>
<img src="/data-project/static/imgs/image3.png" alt="Non-major Boxplot" width="500"/>

## This analysis explores the relationship between student confidence and the demand for supplemental learning tools in COMP110. By quantifying survey data, I investigated whether pre-lecture videos could bridge the gap for students who find introductory programming concepts challenging. 

## In conclusion, the data shows a strong interest (average of 6/7) for pre-lecture videos, especially among students who feel 'Lost' (understanding score < 3). It is my recommendation to implement 5-minute 'Prep' videos for the more difficult units like Recursion. However, a tradeoff is that this increases the workload for the Professors and TAs who have to record and edit these videos.
